Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Micro-and meso-structures and their influence on mechanical properties of selectively laser melted Ti-6Al-4V

Composition_Info.Note: Commercial ELI grade Ti-6Al-4V powder; specific composition values not provided in text

Process_Text.original: Manufactured with ELI grade Ti-6Al-4V powder in an argon gas environment in an EOSINT M 280 SLM unit equipped with a Yb:YAG fiber laser. Process parameters: laser power 280 W, scan speed 1200 mm/s, scan spacing 0.14 mm, layer thickness 30 μm, scan rotation 90° between successive layers, energy density 55.6 J/mm³. Square cross-section coupons (64×64 mm², 14 mm thickness) and cylindrical samples (9 mm diameter, 64 mm long) were produced. Post-SLM stress relief annealing at 650°C for 3 h.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG laser): P=280W, v=1200mm/s, h=0.14mm, t=30μm, φ=90°, J=55.6 J/mm³. Stress relieved at 650°C/3h in argon.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOSINT M 280 SLM unit with Yb:YAG fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Columnar prior β grains with square cross-section in B-plane (side length ~140 μm, approximately equal to scan spacing), spanning the entire height in S-plane. Fine acicular α/α' lath structure within prior β grains, with α phase decorating grain boundaries. Laths oriented at ~45° to build direction due to Burger's orientation relationships between cubic prior β and hexagonal α'. Epitaxial growth results in columnar grains growing in ⟨001⟩β directions normal to deposited layers. High energy density (55.6 J/mm³) melts all powder, eliminating heterogeneous nucleation sites, leading to full-height columnar grain growth. Both α and α' phases present (confirmed by TEM/SAED), with α' having lower c/a ratio (1.58) compared to α (1.59-1.6).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar prior β grains (~140μm, full height), fine acicular α/α' laths at ~45° to BD, α at GBs. Epitaxial growth, no equiaxed grains.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Tensile test, Loading axis parallel to build direction (B)
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Dog-bone, gauge length 6mm, cross-section 2×0.5mm²

Properties_Info.Note: Original paper reports ef (elongation to failure); not distinguished into uniform and fracture components

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Mode I fracture toughness, Loading axis parallel to build direction (B)
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T), 12mm thick, 24mm wide, precracked to a/W~0.45-0.55, fractured at 0.01 mm/s, ASTM E399-12

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: FCG threshold, Loading axis parallel to build direction (B), R=0.1, 10Hz sinusoidal
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T), precracked to a/W~0.45-0.55, ASTM E647-15
Properties_Info.Note: ΔK₀ determined by load shedding method until da/dN=2×10⁻⁹ m/cycle

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Steady-state FCG, Loading axis parallel to build direction (B), R=0.1, 10Hz sinusoidal
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Chevron notch half-sized C(T), ASTM E647-15
Properties_Info.Note: Paris equation: da/dN = C(ΔK)^m

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Rotating bending fatigue (RBF), R=-1 (fully reversed), specimen axis along build direction, survival criterion 10^7 cycles
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: Hourglass RBF specimen, gauge length 22mm, min diameter ~5mm
Properties_Info.Note: FS/σU = 0.28

Process_Text.original: Manufactured with ELI grade Ti-6Al-4V powder in an argon gas environment in an EOSINT M 280 SLM unit equipped with a Yb:YAG fiber laser. Process parameters: laser power 280 W, scan speed 1200 mm/s, scan spacing 0.14 mm, layer thickness 30 μm, scan rotation 67° between successive layers, energy density 55.6 J/mm³. Square cross-section coupons (64×64 mm², 14 mm thickness) and cylindrical samples (9 mm diameter, 64 mm long) were produced. Post-SLM stress relief annealing at 650°C for 3 h.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG laser): P=280W, v=1200mm/s, h=0.14mm, t=30μm, φ=67°, J=55.6 J/mm³. Stress relieved at 650°C/3h in argon.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOSINT M 280 SLM unit with Yb:YAG fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed prior β grains in B-plane (average diameter ~140 μm, equal to scan spacing) due to 67° scan rotation causing mismatch between successive laser tracks. Columnar prior β grains in S-plane but with jagged and discontinuous walls due to laser track mismatch. Fine acicular α/α' lath structure within prior β grains, with α phase at grain boundaries. Both α and α' phases present.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed prior β in B-plane (~140μm), discontinuous columnar in S-plane. Fine acicular α/α' laths, α at GBs.

Process_Text.original: Manufactured with ELI grade Ti-6Al-4V powder in an argon gas environment in an EOSINT M 280 SLM unit equipped with a Yb:YAG fiber laser. Process parameters: laser power 340 W, scan speed 1250 mm/s, scan spacing 0.12 mm, layer thickness 60 μm, scan rotation 90° between successive layers, energy density 37.8 J/mm³. Square cross-section coupons (64×64 mm², 14 mm thickness) and cylindrical samples (9 mm diameter, 64 mm long) were produced. Post-SLM stress relief annealing at 650°C for 3 h.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG laser): P=340W, v=1250mm/s, h=0.12mm, t=60μm, φ=90°, J=37.8 J/mm³. Stress relieved at 650°C/3h in argon.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOSINT M 280 SLM unit with Yb:YAG fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Square-shaped prior β grains in B-plane (side length ~120 μm, reflecting reduced scan spacing of 0.12 mm). Shortened vertical columnar prior β grains in S-plane due to melt zone boundaries from lower energy density (37.8 J/mm³), which prevents full epitaxy. Columnar grains are discontinuous but still aligned along build direction with near-linear boundaries. Fine acicular α/α' lath structure within prior β grains, α at grain boundaries. Possible Ti₃Al phase detected by XRD.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Square prior β in B-plane (~120μm), shortened columnar in S-plane (melt zone boundaries). Fine α/α' laths, possible Ti₃Al.

Process_Text.original: Manufactured with ELI grade Ti-6Al-4V powder in an argon gas environment in an EOSINT M 280 SLM unit equipped with a Yb:YAG fiber laser. Process parameters: laser power 340 W, scan speed 1250 mm/s, scan spacing 0.12 mm, layer thickness 60 μm, scan rotation 67° between successive layers, energy density 37.8 J/mm³. Square cross-section coupons (64×64 mm², 14 mm thickness) and cylindrical samples (9 mm diameter, 64 mm long) were produced. Post-SLM stress relief annealing at 650°C for 3 h.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM (EOSINT M 280, Yb:YAG laser): P=340W, v=1250mm/s, h=0.12mm, t=60μm, φ=67°, J=37.8 J/mm³. Stress relieved at 650°C/3h in argon.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOSINT M 280 SLM unit with Yb:YAG fiber laser

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed prior β grains in B-plane. Irregular and short grain boundaries in S-plane due to combined effect of 67° scan rotation and lower energy density (37.8 J/mm³). Fine acicular α/α' lath structure within prior β grains, with α phase at grain boundaries. Possible Ti₃Al phase detected by XRD. Most equiaxed mesostructure among all four conditions, resulting in optimum combination of high strength, ductility, and fracture toughness.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed prior β in B-plane, irregular short boundaries in S-plane. Fine α/α' laths, possible Ti₃Al. Most isotropic mesostructure.
